name: Winter Vegetable Stew/Pie
recipeIngredient: 3 cups slightly aged chopped white mushrooms
1 cup white mushrooms, slightly aged, halved
1 medium yellow onion, cut into large pieces
1 medium carrot, peeled and cut into 1/2-inch slices
2 medium russet potatoes, halved and cut into 1/2-inch pieces
1 medium fennel bulb, quartered and cut into 1/2-inch slices
2 tbsp fresh thyme, chopped or 2 tsp dried thyme
2 tbsp fresh marjoram, chopped or 2 tsp dried marjoram
2 tbsp fresh parsley, chopped or 2 tsp dried parsley
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the stock by gently simmering it until reduced to about 2 cups liquid.
2. Heat olive oil in a large skillet; add mushrooms and season with salt and pepper. Sear the mushrooms over high heat until golden.
3. Add half the garlic and deglaze the pan with half the sherry. When the pan is almost dry, transfer the mushrooms to a bowl.
4. In the same skillet, heat remaining olive oil in a stew pot; add onion and season with salt and pepper. Sauté until onion is transparent.
5. Add remaining garlic, rutabaga, carrot, potatoes, and fennel. Sauté for 5 minutes.
6. Add mushrooms, herbs, and reduced stock. Cover and simmer until vegetables are soft and flavors melded, about 30 minutes over medium/medium low heat.
7. Serve over grain, pasta, or polenta; add crusty bread, bread sticks, or pretzels.
